evals.prompt: Add example.com to the SEO ranking watchlist with label Main site and target keywords seo audit tool and keyword research. Use the defaults and save it in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Discovers up to 100 current organic keywords, unions them with the supplied targets, performs one successful live rank check per saved keyword, and atomically writes the first snapshot to SEO/example.com/watchlist.json.
assertions: The workflow calls dataforseo_labs_google_ranked_keywords once with limit 100 and organic results, then calls serp_organic_live_advanced once per final keyword.
The domain entry stores label, exact comparison scope, all explicit targets plus up to 100 discovered keywords, and a dated history snapshot with integer or null positions.
Positions use the best matching organic rank_group for the domain or its subdomains, never rank_absolute or 101.
The watchlist is written atomically only after all live calls succeed at SEO/example.com/watchlist.json.

evals.prompt: Check example.com from the saved watchlist and create a full ranking report. The previous positions were seo audit tool 8, ai content tool 12, stable term 3, and lost term 9; current successful live results are 5, 18, 3, and not found within depth 100.
evals.expected_output: Appends the successful snapshot, computes direction and deltas against the immediately previous history entry, highlights the large decline and lost term, surfaces current positions 11-20, recommends one action, and writes the dated Rankink report.
assertions: Movement classifies 8 to 5 as Up (+3), 12 to 18 as Down (-6), 3 to 3 as Same, and 9 to null as Lost.
The short take treats the -6 decline and Lost term as concerning and position 18 as an opportunity just outside the top ten.
The report path follows SEO/example.com/YYYY-MM-DD_Rankink-Report_example.com.md and its first line is the local ISO date.
The report includes scope, watchlist path, movement and current tables, cost log, limitations, and one suggested action.

evals.prompt: List every ranking watchlist under ./SEO, then remove keyword research from example.org. Do not check rankings.
evals.expected_output: Reads the stored JSON files without DataForSEO calls, prints the required summary table, removes only the case-insensitively matched active keyword while retaining history, and ends with a non-evaluated short take.
assertions: The list table contains Domain, Label, # keywords, Last checked, and Last avg position, with null positions excluded from the average.
No DataForSEO call is made for list or remove.
Removing one keyword preserves historical ranking snapshots and unrelated domains.
The output names the resolved store and states that drops and opportunities were not evaluated.

evals.prompt: Check our ranking watchlist again, but use mobile results in Germany this time.
evals.expected_output: Asks for the missing domain and explains that changing the stored comparison scope requires explicit confirmation and a fresh history before making billable calls.
assertions: The workflow asks for the missing domain and waits without making a DataForSEO call.
It does not compare German mobile rankings with a differently scoped saved history.
It requires explicit confirmation before replacing scope and clearing history for a fresh baseline.
